hook.text: Containers changed cloud computing. Now WebAssembly (Wasm) promises to change it again — faster, smaller, more portable.

buildup.text: Wasm is a binary format that runs anywhere — browsers, servers, or edge — at near-native speed, safely sandboxed.

discovery.text: Wasm modules start in microseconds (not milliseconds), use megabytes (not gigabytes), and have no OS dependency.

twist.text: Docker's co-founder said: 'If Wasm existed in 2008, we wouldn't have needed Docker.' That's how big the shift could be.

climax.text: Fermyon, Cosmonic, and Fastly run Wasm in production. It's not replacing containers — it fills gaps they can't.

punchline.text: Wasm starts in microseconds. Docker's creator wishes he had it first.

quiz.question: What advantage does WebAssembly have over containers?
options: Wasm modules start in microseconds and use far less memory than containers
Wasm completely replaces all container technology
Wasm only works in web browsers and has no server-side applications
